economic_notes: Lil'wat Nation has interests in Lil'wat Holdings Limited Partnership (99.99%), Spel'kumtn Community Forest LP, Lil'wat Forestry Ventures LP, Lil'wat Construction Enterprises LP, Lil'wat Retail Operations LP, Lil'wat Management Services LP, Lil'wat Capital Assets LP, and multiple numbered BC companies. Whistler Blackcomb MDA restricted equity of $12.6M for future capital and economic development. BC Hydro settlement funds internally restricted at $3.8M. Baxter Creek Development sale proceeds restricted at $3.6M. BC Gaming Revenue restricted at $1.0M. Subsequent event: acquired 78,685 partnership units in Lil'wat Holdings LP at $10/unit ($786,856) for property purchase at 2027 Highway 99, Pemberton BC.
notes: Business enterprises and partnerships accounted for on modified equity basis including Lil'wat Holdings LP and 9 numbered BC companies and Spel'kumtn Community Forest LP. Segmented information shows Xet'olacw Community School surplus of $11.9M which is the largest driver of annual surplus. Payroll per segmented schedule ($17,877,507) differs slightly from expenses by object wages and benefits ($17,470,698) - difference likely due to classification. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $18,034,150 + CMHC $389,223 + DFO $696,804 + FNESC $2,817,189 + CIRNAC Education Jurisdiction $7,361,441 + FNHA $4,208,983 + BC Gaming $1,019,815 + Province of BC $4,622,134 + ISET $1,384,842 = $40,534,581 (plus some deferred revenue recognized items). No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. The Nation also prepared a separate set of consolidated financial statements for the same year.
